name: Ricobayo Reservoir Loop Trail
name_original: Vuelta de la Embalse de Ricobayo desde Montamarta
description: Moderate 32.9-mile mountain biking loop around Ricobayo reservoir from Montamarta, featuring diverse terrain and panoramic views.
region_intro: Mountain bike trails Montamarta are situated in the province of Zamora, within Spain's Castilla y León region. The area is characterized by its proximity to the Esla River Reservoir, offering scenic routes along its banks. While largely flat, the terrain includes varied elevations, providing diverse mountain biking experiences. This landscape, part of the Tierra del Pan comarca, is suitable for various outdoor activities. Best mountain bike trails around Montamarta The most popular mountain bike trail is Ricobayo Reservoir Loop Trail, a 52.9 miles (85.2 km) trail that takes 4 hours 29 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers extensive views of the Ricobayo Reservoir. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Montamarta - Vía de la Plata Loop, an easy 21.5 miles (34.6 km) path. This route incorporates a section of the historic Vía de la Plata, offering a blend of cultural and natural scenery. Local mountain bikers also love the Ricobayo Reservoir Loop from Palacios del Pan, a 22.2 miles (35.8 km) trail leading through the reservoir area, often completed in about 1 hour 51 minutes. Mountain biking around Montamarta is defined by routes along the Esla River Reservoir, through the generally flat terrain of the Tierra del Pan comarca, and sections of historic paths like the Vía de la Plata. The network offers options for easy to difficult rides, catering to different ability levels. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 140 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Montamarta's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Montamarta?
A: There are &lt;em&gt;11&lt;/em&gt; mountain bike trails around Montamarta, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of mountain bike trails in Montamarta?
A: The trails around Montamarta cater to various skill levels. You'll find &lt;em&gt;3 easy&lt;/em&gt; routes, &lt;em&gt;5 moderate&lt;/em&gt; routes, and &lt;em&gt;3 difficult&lt;/em&gt; routes, ensuring options for beginners to experienced riders.

Q: Are there easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Montamarta?
A: Yes, Montamarta offers several easy mountain bike trails perfect for beginners. An excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018827954/montamarta-via-de-la-plata-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montamarta - Vía de la Plata Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is 21.5 miles (34.6 km) long and incorporates a section of the historic Vía de la Plata.

Q: What about more challenging mountain bike trails for experienced riders near Montamarta?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20149808" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Perilla de Castro – Losacino Bridge loop from Carbajales de Alba&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult route spanning 63.5 miles (102.2 km) with significant elevation changes, offering a demanding ride.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on mountain bike trails around Montamarta?
A: Mountain bike trails around Montamarta offer diverse scenery, primarily characterized by the picturesque Esla River Reservoir. You'll ride through the generally flat terrain of the Tierra del Pan comarca, with occasional varied elevations and scenic views along the reservoir's banks.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural points of interest along the mountain bike routes?
A: Yes, some routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018827954/montamarta-via-de-la-plata-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montamarta - Vía de la Plata Loop&lt;/a&gt;, incorporate sections of the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5441785" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino de la Plata&lt;/a&gt;. Additionally, nearby attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5415854" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castrotorafe Castle (Ruins)&lt;/a&gt; and various religious buildings such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4835252" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Santa María la Real de La Hiniesta&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find viewpoints or scenic spots on the trails around Montamarta?
A: Many trails offer extensive views, especially those that follow the Esla River Reservoir. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1308283187/ricobayo-reservoir-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ricobayo Reservoir Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt; provides expansive vistas of the reservoir, making it ideal for scenic stops.

Q: Are there long-distance mountain bike routes in the Montamarta area?
A: Yes, for those looking for longer rides, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1308283187/ricobayo-reservoir-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ricobayo Reservoir Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a substantial 52.9 miles (85.2 km) route. Another long option is the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20149808" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Perilla de Castro – Losacino Bridge loop from Carbajales de Alba&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 63.5 miles (102.2 km).

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Montamarta?
A: While specific weather conditions vary, the region's climate generally makes spring and autumn ideal for mountain biking in Montamarta. Temperatures are typically milder, and the natural landscape is vibrant, offering comfortable riding conditions.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Montamarta?
A: Yes, several routes are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1308283187/ricobayo-reservoir-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ricobayo Reservoir Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018827954/montamarta-via-de-la-plata-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montamarta - Vía de la Plata Loop&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018301714/ricobayo-reservoir-loop-from-palacios-del-pan" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ricobayo Reservoir Loop from Palacios del Pan&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy the most about mountain biking in Montamarta?
A: Over 140 mountain bikers have explored Montamarta's varied terrain using komoot. Riders often praise the scenic routes along the Esla River Reservoir and the blend of natural beauty with historic paths like the Vía de la Plata.
tips: The hermitage can be accessed by a cement path with a steep slope. Once at the top, there are some benches around the hermitage from where you can enjoy the views of the reservoir.
At this point we are at the Montamarta Town Landmark and next to it, some beautiful views of the Nuestra Señora del Castillo hermitage with the Ricobayo reservoir and the town in the background.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Vuelta de la Embalse de Ricobayo desde Montamarta trail?
faqs.answer: The trail starts from Montamarta. You should be able to find parking within the village to begin your ride. Montamarta is also near a pilgrim's hostel in Granja de Moreruela, which might offer additional parking options.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as moderate. While it is 100% cyclable, it involves undulating terrain with various ascents and descents, and some unpaved sections that can be challenging. It's best suited for well-prepared riders with some mountain biking experience, rather than absolute beginners.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter a diverse mix of surfaces, including local and national roads with light traffic, as well as dirt tracks and trails. The path winds through cereal fields, holm oak forests, cistus shrubs, pastures, and meadows with ash trees and poplars, offering varied riding conditions.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers several scenic highlights. You can enjoy a View of the Hermitage of Nuestra Señora del Castillo, a View of Montamarta and the Church of Nuestra Señora del Castillo, and extensive views of the reservoir from the View of Ricobayo Reservoir from Cañada Real Vía de la Plata. You'll also pass by the Old Manzanal Bridge and the impressive Ricobayo Dam.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: The trail showcases a rich variety of Mediterranean flora, and the reservoir's appearance changes with water levels. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for mountain biking in this region, avoiding the intense heat of summer and potential colder, wetter conditions in winter.

faqs.question: Is the Vuelta de la Embalse de Ricobayo desde Montamarta trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the trail passes through natural areas and rural roads, specific regulations regarding dogs are not provided. It's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially when passing through cultivated fields, near livestock, or on roads, to ensure their safety and respect local wildlife and property.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to ride this mountain bike route around the Ricobayo reservoir. It's a publicly accessible trail.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with other routes?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with other established paths. You will find that it covers parts of the Mtb Vía de la Plata and the Vía de la Plata hiking - 03 Salamanca to Astorga trails.

faqs.question: Are there any historical sites along the trail?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. The route passes by the impressive Ricobayo Dam, a significant 20th-century engineering feat. It also goes near historical sites such as the Monastery of Santa María de Moreruela and the ruins of Castrotorafe, offering a blend of natural beauty and cultural insights.

faqs.question: Is this a loop trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the name 'Vuelta de la Embalse de Ricobayo' translates to 'Loop of the Ricobayo Reservoir', indicating that it is indeed a circular route that brings you back to your starting point in Montamarta.

faqs.question: Are there any specific considerations for riding this trail?
faqs.answer: Be aware that the appearance of the Ricobayo reservoir can change significantly based on water levels, which might affect some views. Also, while some areas offer natural shade from holm oaks and poplars, other sections are exposed, so plan for sun protection. The trail is considered 100% cyclable, but some unpaved segments require attention.
